title: Travel agent — add Ollama as fourth provider via a new mcp/ollama NoETL playbook (Phase 4, mirrors Phase 3)
description: Final missing piece of the multi-provider travel arc. Today: openai (urllib), anthropic (urllib), vertex-ai (via mcp/vertex-ai playbook). Ollama was deferred because there was no NoETL playbook wrapping the in-cluster bridge — only a raw MCP server at `http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8765/jsonrpc`. This round creates `automation/agents/mcp/ollama` as a NoETL playbook exposing the standard `chat_completion` MCP contract (same surface as mcp/vertex-ai), then wires the travel runtime to invoke it via `tool: agent framework: noetl`. The travel runtime's stale `ollama_bridge_url: "...:8080"` workload field gets corrected to port 8765 (the real bridge port — confirmed in troubleshoot/diagnose_execution.yaml line 240). After this round lands, ALL four AI providers route through canonical interfaces (urllib for cloud APIs without significant auth complexity, MCP playbook hops for in-cluster bridges and complex-auth providers). Cap: 1 ops PR + 1 docs PR.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: Travel runtime supports three providers: openai, anthropic (both via urllib in classify_via_http_provider), vertex-ai (via classify_via_vertex_mcp → automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai). Workload has stale `ollama_bridge_url: "http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8080"` — wrong port. Real bridge runs on 8765 (per troubleshoot/diagnose_execution.yaml DEFAULT_OLLAMA_ENDPOINT line 240). The bridge speaks MCP JSON-RPC directly: `POST /jsonrpc` with `{jsonrpc, method, params: {name, arguments}, id}` envelope. No `automation/agents/mcp/ollama` NoETL playbook exists today.
design_brief_for_codex.target_state_part_1_new_playbook: Create `repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml` that mirrors `automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai.yaml` structurally. Metadata: `name: chat_completion`, `path: automation/agents/mcp/ollama`, `agent: true`, `exposes_as_mcp: true`, `exposed_in_ui: false`. Workload: model (default 'gemma3:4b'), messages, system, temperature, ollama_endpoint (default 'http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8765/jsonrpc'). Workflow: one Python step that builds the MCP JSON-RPC envelope, POSTs via urllib, parses the response, extracts text, and returns the standard chat_completion envelope: `{status, content, isError, _meta, usage, data, summary, text}` — identical to mcp/vertex-ai's output shape so callers don't have to differentiate. Use the kind:python urllib pattern (same graceful 5xx handling rationale as the Amadeus MCP playbook).
design_brief_for_codex.target_state_part_2_travel_runtime:
In repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml:

  1. Update workload.ollama_bridge_url from `:8080` to `:8765/jsonrpc`.
  2. Add workload.ollama_model: `gemma3:4b`.
  3. ai_provider comment: `# openai | anthropic | vertex-ai | ollama`.
  4. metadata.capabilities += `ai:ollama`.
  5. Add classify_via_ollama_mcp step (mirrors classify_via_vertex_mcp):
     ```yaml
     - step: classify_via_ollama_mcp
       desc: Classify intent via the Ollama MCP playbook (in-cluster bridge)
       tool:
         kind: agent
         framework: noetl
         entrypoint: automation/agents/mcp/ollama
         payload:
           method: tools/call
           tool: chat_completion
           arguments:
             messages: [{role: user, content: "{{ workload.query }}"}]
             system: <same classifier system prompt as the http and vertex branches>
             temperature: 0
             model: "{{ workload.ollama_model }}"
             ollama_endpoint: "{{ workload.ollama_bridge_url }}"
       next:
         spec: { mode: exclusive }
         arcs:
           - step: classify_intent
     ```
  6. Update start.next.arcs to dispatch `ollama` to the new step:
     ```yaml
     - step: classify_via_http_provider
       when: "{{ workload.ai_provider in ['openai', 'anthropic'] or workload.ai_provider == '' or workload.ai_provider is none }}"
     - step: classify_via_vertex_mcp
       when: "{{ workload.ai_provider == 'vertex-ai' }}"
     - step: classify_via_ollama_mcp
       when: "{{ workload.ai_provider == 'ollama' }}"
     ```
  7. Extend the classify_intent merger to read `classify_via_ollama_mcp | default({})` as a third possible upstream. Extract text the same way as the vertex branch (envelope.text or envelope.content[0].text or envelope.data._meta.text — whatever the new mcp/ollama playbook produces). Strip ```json fences, parse JSON, normalise. On Ollama isError or empty text, fall back to OpenAI with `provider_fallback_reason='ollama <error>'` — same shape as vertex fallback.
design_brief_for_codex.classifier_system_prompt_now_lives_in_th:
After this round, the classifier system_prompt string is duplicated in:
  - classify_via_http_provider's Python `system_prompt` constant
  - classify_via_vertex_mcp's payload `system` argument
  - classify_via_ollama_mcp's payload `system` argument
This is real coupling debt. A future architectural-purity round should extract the prompt into a single workload field (or a tiny preamble step that emits it) referenced by all three. NOT IN SCOPE for this round — but document the coupling clearly in the result file's deferred_followups so the next docs round can pin it.
design_brief_for_codex.no_changes_to_existing_branches: Do NOT modify classify_via_http_provider's body. Do NOT modify classify_via_vertex_mcp's body. Do NOT modify the merger's openai/anthropic or vertex normalisation paths — only ADD the ollama path. The merger's pass-through for the http branch and vertex extraction stay byte-for-byte identical.
design_brief_for_codex.tutorial_07_update: repos/docs/docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md — update Step 3 ('Pluggable AI provider') to add a fourth provider row to whatever table or list shows the providers. Add example commands: `travel --provider ollama help`, `travel --provider ollama flights ...`. Add a paragraph noting Ollama threads through the new mcp/ollama MCP playbook via `tool: agent framework: noetl`, mirroring the Vertex AI pattern. Cite the in-cluster bridge URL.
design_brief_for_codex.no_ollama_provisioning: The ollama-bridge deployment already exists in the cluster (helm chart values.yaml + templates/ollama-bridge-deployment.yaml). Don't redeploy or modify the bridge — just call its existing endpoint.

spec: Reference automation/agents/mcp/vertex-ai.yaml as the structural template.
Workload: model, messages, system, temperature, ollama_endpoint (defaults: gemma3:4b, [], '', 0.1, http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8765/jsonrpc).

Workflow: one Python step (kind:python with urllib) that builds the JSON-RPC envelope `{jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'tools/call', params: {name: 'chat_completion', arguments: {model, messages, system, temperature}}, id: <int>}`, POSTs to ollama_endpoint, parses the response, extracts the assistant text from the MCP result, and returns the standard chat_completion envelope: `{status, content: [{type:'text', text}], isError, _meta: {backend: 'ollama', model, usage, endpoint}, usage, data: {_meta, ollama_response}, summary, text}`. Match mcp/vertex-ai's success and error envelope shapes byte-for-byte where possible — callers are reading those keys.
Use the same `globals().update({...})` pattern from Phase 3 / hotels-activities rounds for helper visibility (NoETL's separate globals/locals model).
Pydantic-validate the new playbook.
1_create_mcp_ollama_playbook.verify: automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml validates as a Playbook resource. Direct invocation should work (will be smoke-tested in phase 4).

2_wire_travel_runtime.objective: Add classify_via_ollama_mcp branch + dispatch + merger extension + workload corrections.
spec: Update workload.ollama_bridge_url from `:8080` to `http://ollama-bridge.noetl.svc.cluster.local:8765/jsonrpc`.
Add workload.ollama_model: `gemma3:4b`.
Update ai_provider comment to include ollama.
metadata.capabilities += ai:ollama.
Add classify_via_ollama_mcp step mirroring classify_via_vertex_mcp.
Update start.next.arcs to dispatch on ai_provider == 'ollama'.

Extend classify_intent merger to handle the ollama branch — read `classify_via_ollama_mcp | default({})`, extract text (same logic as vertex), parse JSON, normalise. On isError/empty: fall back to OpenAI with provider_fallback_reason.
Pydantic-validate the patched runtime.
2_wire_travel_runtime.verify: Travel runtime validates; four-way dispatch in place.

spec: `cd repos/ops && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-ollama-via-mcp`.
Stage automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml AND automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml.

Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `feat(travel-agent): add Ollama as fourth provider via new mcp/ollama MCP playbook (Phase 4)`. Body: cites Phase 3 mcp/vertex-ai pattern; explains the new playbook wraps the existing in-cluster bridge; corrects the stale port; notes Ollama enables cheap-first inference and pairs with the troubleshoot agent's existing mcp/ollama default.
PR title: same. Push, PR, merge with `--squash --delete-branch`.

4_open_and_merge_docs_pr.objective: Tutorial 07 mentions ollama as fourth provider.
spec: `cd repos/docs && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-tutorial-ollama-via-mcp`.
Edit docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md — extend Step 3 with the fourth provider; add example commands; mention the in-cluster bridge URL and the new mcp/ollama MCP playbook.
Verify: `cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build` clean.
Commit: `docs(tutorials): note ollama as fourth provider via the mcp/ollama MCP playbook (Phase 4)`.
Push, PR, merge.

5_re_register_and_smoke.objective: Register both playbooks; smoke ollama provider; spot-check existing providers.
spec: `noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml` (new playbook — capture catalog version 1).
`noetl register repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml` (catalog v23 expected).
Smoke `travel --provider ollama help` — expect status=completed, effective_provider='ollama', no provider_fallback_reason, widget rendered.
Smoke `travel --provider ollama flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — same; expect render_flights or render_amadeus_failure widget.
Smoke `travel --provider ollama locations near Boston`.
Smoke `travel --provider ollama hotels in Paris on 2026-08-15`.
Smoke `travel --provider ollama activities near Times Square`.

Verify the agent → MCP hop event: `noetl status <id> --json | jq '.events[] | select(.step | test("classify_via_ollama_mcp")) | {step, type: .tool_kind, sub_execution: .sub_execution_id}'`. Expected: tool_kind='agent', sub_execution_id present.
Verify the ollama child execution itself completes: `noetl status <ollama_sub_execution_id> --json | jq '{status, isError: .result.isError, model: .result._meta.model, usage: .result._meta.usage}'`.
Spot-check regression: `travel help` (default → openai) and `travel --provider vertex-ai help` — both clean.
Verify travel_agent_events audit row shows ai_provider='ollama' for the ollama smokes.
Direct external MCP smoke: `cd /mcp/ollama; tools; call chat_completion messages='[{"role":"user","content":"ping"}]' model=gemma3:4b` — returns a clean text response.
Capture screenshots.
5_re_register_and_smoke.verify: All five ollama intents complete with effective_provider='ollama'. Regressions clean. Direct MCP works.

6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.objective: Bump repos/ops + repos/docs gitlinks in ai-meta. Stage but do not push.
spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/ops repos/docs`.
`git add repos/ops repos/docs`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump ops + docs for travel ollama via mcp playbook (Phase 4)`.
Don't push ai-meta — Kadyapam pushes.
6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit.

expected_state.description: automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml exists and exposes chat_completion. Travel runtime supports `--provider ollama` for all five intents with effective_provider='ollama'. The mcp/vertex-ai playbook is unchanged. The 'MCP is just a playbook' thesis has a third concrete example (Amadeus + Vertex AI + Ollama).
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; HAS_OLLAMA_PB=$(test -f repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/ollama.yaml && echo 1 || echo 0); HAS_OLLAMA_BRANCH=$(grep -c 'classify_via_ollama_mcp' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_OLLAMA_MODEL=$(grep -c 'ollama_model:' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); CORRECTED_PORT=$(grep -c '8765/jsonrpc' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "playbook:$HAS_OLLAMA_PB branch:$HAS_OLLAMA_BRANCH model:$HAS_OLLAMA_MODEL port:$CORRECTED_PORT"; if [ "$HAS_OLLAMA_PB" -ge 1 ] && [ "$HAS_OLLAMA_BRANCH" -ge 1 ] && [ "$HAS_OLLAMA_MODEL" -ge 1 ] && [ "$CORRECTED_PORT" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: TRAVEL_OLLAMA_VIA_MCP_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/gui, repos/e2e — ops + docs only round
Modifying the Amadeus or Vertex AI MCP playbooks (single sources of truth hold)
Modifying classify_via_http_provider or classify_via_vertex_mcp bodies — only ADD ollama branch
Modifying the merger's openai/anthropic or vertex normalisation paths — only ADD ollama path
Re-deploying or modifying the ollama-bridge helm chart
preferred_tools: cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
Browser screenshot for the prompt smokes
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-001500-travel-ollama-via-mcp.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_create_mcp_ollama_playbook: {validates, mirrors_vertex_ai_shape: bool}, 2_wire_travel_runtime: {validates}, 3_open_and_merge_ops_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 4_open_and_merge_docs_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, docs_build_clean}, 5_re_register_and_smoke: {ollama_catalog_id, ollama_version, travel_catalog_id, travel_version, help_status, flights_status, locations_status, hotels_status, activities_status, all_five_effective_provider_ollama: bool, openai_default_regression: bool, vertex_ai_regression: bool, agent_step_proven_in_events: bool, ollama_child_completed: bool, audit_rows_show_ollama: bool, screenshots: []}, 6_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {ops_sha, docs_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Single-source-of-truth refactor for classifier system_prompt — now duplicated across THREE branches (http/vertex/ollama)', 'NoETL-Python globals/locals idiom into the authoring guide as 13th rule', 'Anthropic re-smoke gated on user provisioning the GCP secret in noetl-demo-19700101', 'Investigate Amadeus test API 500s on flights/locations']}. If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
